action.reasoning: Observation: Iteration 20 scored 0.6072 (88.7% success, 346.4m), just shy of the best-ever 0.6118 (92% success, 354.8m). The current predict.py code is truncated in context but functional on disk. Room success is the weak point (0.78 vs 0.86 in the best run). Analysis: We have ~79 minutes and 10 iterations left. To beat 0.6118, I need to boost success rate, especially in rooms, even if distance increases slightly. The best lever is tuning the distance denominator (dist_denom) and adding score persistence to reduce oscillation. Before burning an eval iteration, I'll run a quick local sweep over 4 distance denominators and 2 reverse-penalty settings on 9 training maps (3 per type) to find the most promising config. Decision: Rewrite predict.py completely with a clean GreedyPlanner (global Dijkstra, utility/(dist+denom), anti-backtracking, persistence bonus, multi-tier fallback, no hard hop filter). Write quick_tune.py to compare 6 configs locally. Run train + smoke test + quick tune in one step.
action.command: python train.py && python smoke_test.py && python quick_tune.py
